title: ASM Solution 2
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"asam8","text":"First, prepare primary stock solutions. Glassware should be acid-washed and rinsed well with dH2O before use. If unable to measure small quantities accurately, either scale up the recipe or prepare additional stock solutions of the individual compounds.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":95,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8f13v","text":"Prepare primary stock solutions (in dH2O) of the following:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":38,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6hfnn","text":"NaNO3: 75.00 g/L","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89no1","text":"NaH2PO4·H2O: 5.00 g/L","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":3,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":9,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ado6b","text":"Na2SiO3·9HO: 30.00 g/L (Note: this will only be used if phytoplankton require silicates)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":2,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bqp1o","text":"Filter sterilize through a 0.2 micron PES membrane and store frozen in 1 mL aliquots.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissolve 4.796 g MgCl 2 ·6H 2 O into bottle.
Dissolve 0.672 g CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O into bottle.
Dissolve 10.95 mg SrCl 2 ·6H 2 O into bottle.
NaNO 3 : 1 ml primary stock solution (75.00 g/L)
NaH 2 PO 4 ·H 2 O: 1 ml primary stock solution (5.00 g/L)
Na 2 SiO 3 · 9H 2 O (optional): 1 ml primary stock solution (30.00 g/L) Only add if phytoplankton requires silicates.
Measure 500 mL dH 2 O into a 1 L glass bottle.
Mix final solution well and set aside until ready to autoclave.
creator: Christa Smith
